summary: The implementation fails to properly sanitize the container_name input against directory traversal sequences (e.g., '..'), allowing an attacker to write files outside the intended /var/app/metadata/ directory. Despite three attempts (problem_solver + two security_fixer iterations), the same codeql:py/path-injection finding persisted because the fix only replaced '/' and '\' but did not block '..' sequences. The code otherwise correctly handles CSRF validation and command injection prevention.
suggested_actions: Add explicit validation to reject container_name containing '..' or path separators after URL decoding
Use os.path.abspath() and os.path.commonpath() to ensure the resolved path stays within /var/app/metadata/
Consider using a whitelist of allowed container names from the dropdown instead of sanitizing user input
Apply both allowlist-based validation and path containment checks to eliminate the path traversal vulnerability

evidence.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
